title: Pacific Biosciences (PACB) Reports Record Consumable Revenue in Q1 2026
text: Pacific Biosciences reported record consumable revenue in Q1 2026 reaching $21.8 million, representing 9% year-over-year growth. The record consumables performance highlighted the company's transition toward a recurring revenue model driven by clinical adoption. Total Q1 2026 revenue was $37.2 million, flat year-over-year, as consumable strength was offset by weaker instrument sales at $9.7 million down 12% year-over-year. Management noted strong clinical adoption with shipments to clinical and hospital customers showing over 100% year-over-year growth. Clinical accounts now represent a mid-teens percentage of total consumable shipments, doubling from prior year. The company faces headwinds from softer academic funding and Vega instrument demand. However, management is optimistic about SparkNex launch and continued clinical expansion driving 2026 growth. SPRQ-Nx chemistry and improved product mix are expected to drive non-GAAP gross margin expansion to 41-44% range in 2026.
